I was implementing the on-chain `>2/3` K-of-N validator-approval flow in `gilt-consensus` and validating it with formatting/tests.

Facts:
- I changed stake code so `approve-validator` is validator voting (not single admin), with snapshot power, one vote per validator, and finalization only when `YES > 2/3`.
- I gated `validator-join`, stake updates, and signer updates so they fail while approval is still pending.
- I updated stake tests and genesis validation around this flow.
- I ran `gofmt` and `go test ./x/stake/...` while doing that.

Why this matters: it removes single-key validator admission and aligns admission control with the chain’s BFT trust model.
